generated_from: manual code audit (compiled_adapters.py / adapters.py `kind == "tuple"` branches) cross-checked against real ledger evidence for min-stack-simulation (the only tuple-typed problem in the corpus), not inferred from naming or prose claims
note: A crude text-search for the word 'tuple' in each adapter class is UNRELIABLE (false positives from comments/raised-error messages that mention 'tuple' while explicitly NOT supporting it) -- this matrix is grounded in (a) the real atlascode_matrix_ledger row for min-stack-simulation per language, and (b) direct confirmation in source of *why* for the 3 unsupported languages.

supported.python: native tuple -> JSON array, no special-case needed
supported.javascript: native tuple -> JSON array, no special-case needed
supported.typescript: reuses JS's dynamic/untyped driver convention -- a tuple is just an array, works for free, no dedicated tuple branch in the adapter
supported.java: boxed Object[] stream decode -- no native Java tuple, represented as heterogeneous Object[]
supported.csharp: native ValueTuple -- direct fit, real JSON parser (System.Text.Json)
supported.perl: array-of-mixed-scalars via arrayref -- works naturally, no dedicated struct needed
supported.ruby: JSON array is just a Ruby Array -- works for free, no dedicated tuple branch
supported.php: array-of-mixed-scalars, PHP arrays are naturally heterogeneous
supported.r: list()-based, no dedicated tuple branch needed
supported.rust: generated per-contract struct/tuple type -- direct fit

kotlin.limit_reason: "Kotlin Function Mode only supports 2-element tuples (via Pair) -- corpus audit found no wider tuple shape" (compiled_adapters.py:2664) -- a real, current-corpus-driven limit, not a gap to necessarily fix until a 3+-element tuple problem exists

scala.support: PARTIAL -- 2-element only, via native (A, B) tuple
scala.limit_reason: "Scala Function Mode only supports 2-element tuples -- corpus audit found no wider tuple shape" (compiled_adapters.py:3003), same reasoning as Kotlin

c.reason: No generics, no native product type -- representing array<tuple<string, optional<integer>>> would need a new hand-rolled helper per tuple shape
c.code_reference: compiled_adapters.py:1506 -- raises ValueError("C Function Mode does not support tuple-typed contracts yet")

go.reason: No native tuple type, same generics-free limitation as C
go.code_reference: compiled_adapters.py:2430 -- raises ValueError("Go Function Mode does not support tuple-typed contracts yet")

swift.reason: Swift tuples aren't Equatable/Codable/storable in a way the JSON encode/decode and comparison pipeline can use generically
swift.code_reference: compiled_adapters.py:3351 -- raises ValueError("Swift Function Mode does not support tuple-typed contracts yet")

not_applicable.shell: Function Mode is an architectural exclusion entirely (see registry.py SHELL_FUNCTION_MODE_POLICY) -- tuple support is moot
